title_ar: رسائل ابن تيمية من السجن: الجزء الثاني
title_id: Bagian 2: Keistimewaan Ibnu Taimiyyah dan Kepeduliannya terhadap Umat
content_id:
Para sarjana zaman itu dengan cepat mengakui kelebihannya, sebagaimana al-Qadi al-Zamalakani memberikan deskripsi yang tepat: "Sebagaimana Allah telah melembutkan besi untuk Dawud, Dia telah melenturkan ilmu-ilmu agar mudah dikuasai oleh Ibnu Taimiyyah." Mengapa Ibnu Taimiyyah merupakan sosok yang begitu luar biasa, seseorang mungkin bertanya. Pertama, ada ikatan yang konstan dan tak terputus dengan massa umat Muslim, karena beliau adalah guru dan pembimbing mereka, beliau akan menyelesaikan masalah-masalah mereka dan membela hak-hak mereka di hadapan para penguasa. Beliau akan berusaha mempertahankan keteguhan mereka ketika musuh-musuh mereka menyerang, beliau akan memerintahkan yang baik dan melarang yang mungkar, dan yang paling penting, beliau tidak terganggu oleh urusan-urusan duniawi yang fana. Oleh karena itu, seluruh waktunya didedikasikan untuk menuntut ilmu dan berpartisipasi dalam jihad. Memang, ikatan yang kuat inilah yang membuat massa masyarakat Damaskus mencintai, menghormati, dan memuliakannya. Bahkan yang paling cemburu dari musuh-musuhnya tidak dapat mencelakakannya di sana, tetapi mereka punya kesempatan di Mesir di mana beliau tidak begitu dikenal.

Perasaan tulus terhadap urusan-urusan umat Muslim ini dapat dirasakan ketika Syaikhul Islam berbicara tentang politik: "Peradaban berakar pada keadilan, dan konsekuensi penindasan sangat dahsyat. Oleh karena itu, dikatakan bahwa Allah membantu negara yang adil meskipun non-Muslim, namun menahan pertolongan-Nya dari negara yang opresif meskipun Muslim." Beliau juga berkata: "Ada Muslim-Muslim yang tulus yang memandang bahwa menduduki jabatan tinggi pasti mengarah pada kecintaan terhadap kekuasaan dan kekayaan. Beberapa Muslim awam menganggap orang yang menerima tanggung jawab demikian sebagai berpaling dari 'agama belas kasih dan kerendahan hati'. Namun, sikap yang benar adalah bahwa pengangkatan orang-orang yang saleh jauh lebih baik melayani umat daripada menyerahkan jabatan kepada orang-orang yang fasik." Kepeduliannya juga terhadap masyarakat dalam masalah-masalah ekonomi, menyerang orang-orang yang mendirikan monopoli atas bahan makanan: "Di masa-masa kebutuhan, penguasa dapat memaksa orang-orang menjual barang-barang mereka dengan harga aslinya!"

Ketika Tatar mendekati Damaskus, rasa takut mencekam penduduk dan beberapa orang berpikir untuk melarikan diri. Namun Ibnu Taimiyyah menolak ide-ide yang menyerah ini dan sebaliknya ia mengajak orang-orang untuk tidak pergi dan untuk teguh. Beliau berkata sebagai semangat kepada para jenderal pasukan: "Allah akan memberi kita kemenangan!" dan mereka merespons dengan memintanya untuk mengatakan "Insya Allah", tetapi beliau menjawab: "Aku mengatakannya dengan keyakinan dan bukan sekadar harapan!" Memang, beliau berpartisipasi dalam jihad melawan Tatar dalam pertempuran Shaqhab setelah mengumumkan fatwanya yang terkenal yang menyatakan Tatar sebagai kafir, karena keteguhan mereka pada pengabaian beberapa syiar Islam, meskipun mereka mengucapkan syahadat. Ketika seorang ulama dipenjara dan kabar itu mencapai Ibnu Taimiyyah, beliau secara pribadi pergi dan berhasil membebaskan ulama tersebut, setelah memuji dan membelanya di hadapan penguasa Damaskus.
content_en: Make me comprehend.'" His contemporaries were quick to recognise his merit, as al-Qadi az-Zamalkaanee gave a true description, "Just as Allaah had made iron soft for Daawood, He made sciences supple for Ibn Taymeeyah's grasp." Why was Ibn Taymeeyah such a distinguished figure, one might ask. Firstly, there was his constant and unbroken bond with the masses of Muslims, for he was their teacher and mentor, he would resolve their problems and defend their rights in the face of the rulers. He would try to keep them steadfast when their enemies attacked, he would enjoin the good and forbid the evil, and most especially, he was not diverted by mundane worldly matters. Therefore, the whole of his time was devoted to attaining knowledge and participating in jihads. Indeed, it was this strong connection that made the general masses of Damascus love, respect and honour him. Even the most jealous of his enemies were not able to harm him there, but instead they had their chance in Egypt where he was not as well known. These sincere feelings for the affairs of Muslims can be sensed when Shaykh ul-lslaam spoke regarding politics, "Civilisation is rooted in justice, and the consequences of oppression are devastating. Therefore, it is said that Allaah aids the just state even if it is non-Muslim, yet withholds His help from the oppressive state even if it is Muslim." He also said,"There are sincere Muslims who perceive that commanding a high post 3 inevitably leads to love of rule and wealth. Some common Muslims regard the acceptor of such responsibility, as a turning away from the 'religion of mercy and humbleness'. However, the correct attitude is that the appointment of the virtuous serves the Ummah far better than assigning posts to the wicked." His 2 A famous book that Imaam ash-shawkaanee explained in his Nayl ul-Awtaar. 3 E.g. a judge, a minister, or an administrator. concern was also with the public in economic problems, attacking those who establish monopolies over foodstuffs, "In times of need, the ruler can force people to sell their goods at their original value!" As the Tatars were approaching Damascus, fear gripped the population and some thought of fleeing. Yet Ibn Taymeeyah rejected such defeatist ideas and instead, he appealed for the people not to depart and to be steadfast. He would say in encouragement to the generals of the army, "Allaah will grant us victory!" and they would respond by asking him to say, "Insh'Allaah," but he would reply, "I say it in certainty and not in mere hope!" Indeed, he participated in the jihaad against the Tatars in the battle of Shaqhab after announcing his famous Fatwa declaring the Tatars kaffir, due to their insistence upon the abandonment of some of the rites of Islaam, even though they pronounced the Shahaadah. When one of the scholars was imprisoned, and the news reached Ibn Taymeeyah, he personally went and managed the scholar's release, after praising and vindicating him in front of the ruler of Damascus. In another instance, he heard of a man who blasphemed against the Prophet (salallaahu alayhi wa salam), so he stood to forbid the evil, and with the masses supporting him, he wrote the famous book, as-Sarim al-Maslool 'ala Shatem ar-Rasool 4. Furthermore, his deep concern for the Muslims, and his intimate knowledge of their affairs in every country, their conditions, and their nearness or distance to Islaam stands out. This is illustrated in his description of the Muslims in the lands of Sham 5 and Egypt who, were standing firm at his time, defending their lands. " lf one is to review the affairs of the world, one would inevitably realise that this group in ashsham and Egypt are the most staunch group upholding the Deen in knowledge, action and jihads. They are relieving the Muslims throughout the world of their obligation of jihaad as they struggle against the hardened disbelievers. The prestige of all Muslims is derived from that group's glory. . . "For the inhabitants of Yemen are weak, and unable or unwilling to carry out jihads, subservient to their rulers, "The Hijaazi peoples are swamped in the depths of innovations and misguidance, and their people of knowledge and faith are weak and subdued. lf that group in ash-Sham and Egypt were to be subjugated - and l seek refuge in Allah from thatthen those from Hijaaz would be rendered the most degraded of Allaah's servants. "The lands of Africa 6 are led by its Bedouins and they are very wicked, and themselves deserving to be conquered by jihaad. Further on, the lands of the Maghreb are all but occupied by the Europeans, yet Muslims there do not attempt their jihads. Had Tatars occupied those regions, they would have encountered timid people. . . 4 Meaning, "The Drawn Sword on the Blasphemer of the Prophet.” 5 The lands of ash-Sham refer to the areas that were historically under the administrational Damascus, Syria, They include today's Syria, Lebanon, Palestine and Jordan. 6 The term 'lands of Africa' refers to today's Libya, Tunisia and Algeria.
content_ar:
كتب ابن تيمية رحمه الله وهو في سجن القلعة بمصر رسائل تتدفق منها أحاسيس المؤمن الصادق المتوكل على الله. يُخبر أصحابه وتلاميذه أنه في أحسن حال وأنعم بال، وأن ما يجده من العلم والذكر في سجنه لا يستبدله بشيء. وكان يكتب: «إن الله يُعوّضني في هذا السجن بما هو خير لي مما لو كنت طليقاً». وهذا ليس تعبيراً مصطنعاً للتماسك، بل هو ترجمة صادقة لتجربة روحية عميقة عاشها.

وقد تضمنت هذه الرسائل إلى جانب هذا التعبير الروحي الشخصي توجيهات علمية دقيقة لأصحابه في مسائل كانوا يستفتونه فيها، وتنبيهات على مواطن الغلط في العقيدة والعمل، وتحذيرات من الفتن والانحرافات المنتشرة في عصره. وهكذا جمعت هذه الرسائل بين الوعظ والتعليم والتأسي في قالب إنساني بالغ الأثر.
